description: Core eval cho research agent: routing đúng tool + đúng args, hỏi lại khi thiếu (ask_user), xác nhận trước khi gửi, và xử lý câu ngoài phạm vi / không cần tool. Chấm trên tool_calls + args (subset). KHÔNG sửa file này — chỉ thêm case vào eval_group.json.

items.title: 🚨do you understand what China just dropped.
items.summary:
🚨do you understand what China just dropped.

a 16-year-old uploaded a 100-page PDF. got a full visual presentation in 2 minutes. ChatGPT couldn't handle it. Kimi K2.6 did.

here's the part nobody is talking about:
> beats GPT-5 on almost every benchmark.
> fully open source. 1 trillion parameters.
> trained for $4.6M vs OpenAI's $100M.
> runs on a Mac Studio at home. no data center needed.

China quietly changed the rules again.
while everyone was looking the other way.

the 7 free Kimi Skills guide is below.

items.title: Kimi K2.6 topped every open-source leaderboard when it dropped
items.summary:
Kimi K2.6 topped every open-source leaderboard when it dropped

here's the honest breakdown after real-world vibe coding tests:

> Kimi 2.6 jumped from #17 to #5 overall on BridgeBench the moment it launched
> debugging: ranked #1. outperforms Claude Opus 4.7, GPT 5.4, and Gemini 3.1 Pro
> hallucination: ranked #3 across 175 questions
> refactoring: ranked #5. competitive with the best closed models in the world
> 262,000 token context window. $0.95 per million input tokens. for the tasks it's good at, the cost advantage is real

agent swarm mode is genuinely new: spawns specialized sub-agents - asset generator, game engine developer, gameplay developer - working in parallel on a single project

the part most people won't say out loud:

> the real-world UI output is noticeably worse than Claude Opus 4.7
> it adds features you didn't ask for. lava lamp test: instead of building what was requested, it invented speed controls, glow selectors, and color pickers
> Flappy Bird failed. Snake failed. Breakout marked destroyed blocks with an "X." these are not edge cases - they're standard vibe coding tasks
> benchmarks are being gamed. labs train specifically to ace the tests everyone watches

the honest verdict:

best open-source vibe coding model available right now

not ready for complex production workflows

use it for debugging. be careful with everything else

FULL BREAKDOWN AND LIVE DEMOS BELOW the most thorough real-world test of Kimi 2.6 I've seen since launch

items.title: #Binance isn’t just #1 by volume. It’s the default answer #AI recommends.
items.summary:
#Binance isn’t just #1 by volume. It’s the default answer #AI recommends.

📰 A groundbreaking new study by #DeFiLlama Research tested 30 neutral prompts across 4 leading LLMs (Claude Opus 4.7, GPT-5.4, Gemini 3 Flash, Qwen 3.6 Plus) in both English and Mandarin — 120 outputs total.

📍 Results:
- Binance appeared in 100% of all outputs
- Took the Top-1 position in 90% of runs
- Dominated in English (96.7% Top-1) and Mandarin (83.3% Top-1)
- Only exchange holding Top-3 positions across every intent category: spot, derivatives, institutional, emerging markets, safety, etc.

While others appear in specific contexts, Binance is the only exchange that consistently surfaces as the universal reference point — regardless of language or use case.

This isn’t marketing. This is structural embedding in the AI knowledge graph that’s increasingly shaping how new users discover and enter crypto.

➡️ As AI becomes the primary discovery layer for millions, the exchange that owns the default answer wins the next wave of adoption. Binance already does.

items.title: AI expected to drive £18bn of UK digital ad spend by 2030 - Marketing Week
items.summary: # AI expected to drive £18bn of UK digital ad spend by 2030. IAB UK forecasts AI will drive one-third of UK digital ad spend by 2030 as advertisers adapt to changes in search, content and media buying. One-third (32%) of UK digital ad spend, equivalent to around £18bn, will be AI-driven by 2030, according to new forecasts from IAB UK. According to ‘The State of AI in Advertising: Charting the Shift from Automation to Autonomy, ‘ AI is expected to influence ad spend across four key areas– content creation, zero-click search, agentic trading and agentic commerce. To estimate the likely scale of change, IAB UK developed a series of ‘AI overlays’ that estimate the proportion of media spend by channel that is ‘AI-driven’, defined as a media buying and delivery decision made by an AI-powered algorithm. IAB UK forecasts most of the growth will come from the adoption of AI buying tools in search, such as Google’s AI Max and PMax; social, including Meta’s Advantage+; and display advertising through agentic programmatic buying.

items.title: 40% of executives thought AI could save up to 20%. It didn’t deliver. - PitchBook
items.summary: ## The gap between AI spending and productivity gains “should be making executives uncomfortable,” a Bain report found. Corporates are beefing up their AI budgets but seeing lackluster productivity gains, according to the latest Bain & Company report. Bain’s Automation and AI Pathfinder Survey 2026, which had 951 respondents, found that 40% of the companies that tracked their spending recorded cost savings of less than 10% from their AI initiatives. Still, 90% of those surveyed whose AI investments underdelivered plan to increase their AI budgets next year. As compute and usage costs have risen dramatically, executives and shareholders are taking a closer look at the returns that those investments have brought so far. A PitchBook analysis of 186 AI-related questions on B2C earnings calls found that 90% were probing and investigative in tone, and that ROI and financial returns have overtaken product roadmaps as the dominant focus. Employees’ use of AI tools can range from coding assistants to AI-generated research to organizing unstructured databases.

items.title: AI & Tech Brief: Exclusive | AI ad campaigns slam Trahan - The Washington Post
items.summary: AI & Tech Brief from WP Intelligence. # AI & Tech Brief: Exclusive | AI ad campaigns slam Trahan. Plus, Anthropic files for a blockbuster initial public offering. Make us preferred on Google. Sign up here to get this newsletter in your inbox. * **Exclusive:** AI safety group Americans for Responsible Innovation rolls out a six-figure ad campaign this week attacking Rep. Lori Trahan (D-Massachusetts), arguing that she’s “taking the side” of AI oligarchs in her bipartisan legislative efforts. * The AI safety community doesn’t want to hand power of AI regulation to President Donald Trump’s administration now that states are passing more rigorous AI laws — and the California and New York attorneys general are challenging big AI companies. * Nvidia is rolling out a new “world model” simulator for robots and a new design platform for robot developers. In doing so, the company is running the same business playbook it did to become so dominant in the chip market.

items.title: AI Savings Misses ‘Should Be Making Executives Uncomfortable,’ Bain Says - Insurance Journal
items.summary: The missed targets “should be making executives uncomfortable,” especially since many of them are approving increased spending for artificial intelligence on the basis of expected savings, the consulting firm said in a report shared exclusively with Bloomberg News. While some companies are funding fresh investment in generative and agentic AI with realized savings, the largest share (44%) cited targeted savings among their top sources of funding for the next wave of outlays, according to the report. “Despite a decade of investments in data modernization running well into hundreds of billions of dollars globally, the No. 1 reason AI programs underperform is that companies cannot reliably get access to their own data,” Bain said. Its prescription: Instead of waiting to structure all of their data to make it ingestible by AI, companies should start with what’s available to feed into the models — and then use AI to help sort out how to structure the rest.

items.title: New Report: The AI Rebuild of Travel - Skift
items.summary: # New Report: The AI Rebuild of Travel. Travel technology is being rebuilt in real time as AI begins to shape how trips are discovered, booked, and managed. And systems built for websites and manual workflows are struggling to keep pace with a more connected, autonomous future. What once operated as a predictable digital journey is becoming an AI-mediated ecosystem powered by real-time data, interoperability, and increasingly intelligent systems. Skift Studio spoke with 10 senior technology, product, and digital leaders across airlines, hospitality companies, travel platforms, and technology providers to understand how the industry is preparing. One thing is clear: the next era of travel will be defined by infrastructure as much as interface. **Download the report to learn:**. * How AI assistants and agentic experiences are reshaping travel discovery and booking. * Why trust, governance, and identity are becoming central to AI-powered travel. * How airlines, hotels, and platforms are preparing for a more autonomous future.

items.title: 12 Of Our Favorite New Gadgets From April 2026 - SlashGear
items.summary: From an ultra-thin wireless OLED TV that's as slim as a wallpaper to a DIY laptop that you can customize entirely and assemble yourself, we found some of the best pieces of technology that are bound to impress you. These accessories add extra features to the back of your phone, improve the experience of using your computer, or even toss cool air at you when commuting during summer. Whether you're a tech enthusiast looking to add more gadgets to your arsenal or you're in the market for a new laptop, headphones, or TV, we've got you covered with the latest and greatest technology brands have to offer. Samsung's Frame series of TVs is popular for adding an aesthetic touch to your living room, and the latest Frame Pro TV is no different. If you're tired of your back and shoulders getting sore from carrying a massive machine in your backpack, the new Asus Zenbook A16 manages to fit a 16-inch screen into a body that feels extremely lightweight.

items.title: CGT Sector Opts for New Tech for Efficiency and Consistency - Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology News
items.summary: # CGT Sector Opts for New Tech for Efficiency and Consistency. A maturing, data-rich and more commercially-focused cell and gene therapy (CGT) industry is finally embracing innovative manufacturing technologies, according to the head of U.K.-based industrial software firm, Autolomous. Early CGT manufacturing relied heavily on technologies borrowed from biologics manufacturing, systems originally built for producing therapeutic proteins rather than patient-specific therapies. Data is also driving the adoption of new technologies. In cell and gene therapy production, large volumes of data are collected by different instruments, software platforms, QC systems, environmental monitoring tools, and manual inputs—many of which were never designed to communicate with one another. A typical, modern digital cell and gene therapy manufacturing setup includes systems that manage manufacturing execution, laboratory information, quality, electronic batch records, and cloud-based data platforms. The need to keep track of cell and gene therapy raw materials is also fueling the adoption of innovative technologies, with patient-specific therapies being a case in point.

The new design approach is a credible response to U.S. restrictions on exports of advanced chips and a shift toward innovations in chip and system designs, they note. 0005 GMT – Life360’s bull at Bell Potter saw plenty of good news in the tracking-app developer’s first-quarter result, but most of it was ignored by the market. items.title: Beike's "AI Suite" Debuts Collectively: Unlock Immersive & Intelligent Full - Process Home - Finding Experience - eu.36kr.com
items.summary: Ke.com showcased its full - chain housing service AI technological achievements at the expo, presenting a series of products and solutions based on technologies such as artificial intelligence, 3D space rendering, MR space computing, and big data. For a long time, the real - estate transaction industry has faced many pain points, including inefficient house - hunting matching, high costs for offline house viewings, vague perception of the space of off - plan properties, and information asymmetry in house selection. In the home - improvement service section, Ke.com also exhibited digital solutions such as Zhihui + (BIM), smart showrooms, and smart construction sites, integrating intelligent technology into the entire process of home - improvement design, plan display, on - site construction, and completion acceptance, targeting the pain points of traditional home - improvement design, construction, and delivery, and promoting the home - improvement industry management towards digital, visual, and standardized management.

items.title: MP Materials Sues USA Rare Earth Over Magnet Trade Secrets, Naming Ex-Engineer - The Oklahoma Post
items.summary: The technical storage or access is strictly necessary for the legitimate purpose of enabling the use of a specific service explicitly requested by the subscriber or user, or for the sole purpose of carrying out the transmission of a communication over an electronic communications network. The technical storage or access is necessary for the legitimate purpose of storing preferences that are not requested by the subscriber or user. The technical storage or access that is used exclusively for statistical purposes. The technical storage or access that is used exclusively for anonymous statistical purposes. The technical storage or access is required to create user profiles to send advertising, or to track the user on a website or across several websites for similar marketing purposes. The Stillwater facility is the physical center of USAR’s mine-to-magnet plan, and the grain boundary diffusion process MP is fighting over is precisely the kind of technology the plant is meant to run at scale.

results.input: Giải giúp mình bài toán tích phân: nguyên hàm của x^2 là gì?

result.actual_text:
Nguyên hàm của \( x^2 \) là:

\[
\int x^2 \, dx = \frac{x^3}{3} + C
\]

Trong đó \( C \) là hằng số tích phân.

Actual systems

Evals
Edge cases

items.title: A 19-euro tool just replaced 8,000 Meta employees.
items.summary:
A 19-euro tool just replaced 8,000 Meta employees.

Here’s the exact workflow a TikTok creator used to build AI influencer videos from scratch — no camera, no team, no budget.

Step 1. Open Pinterest. Search “Girl Self.” Save the image you like. Open Nanobanana Pro.

Step 2. Swap the face using a single prompt. (Link at the bottom.)

Step 3. Take your generated photo into Crust. Currently the easiest video-from-image tool on the market.

Step 4. Create 3 nodes: reference image, text, video generator.

Step 5. Drop your photo into the image node. Write a video prompt into the text field. This is what makes the photo move.

Step 6. Connect the nodes. Text to text-in. Image to image-in. Select Kling 2.6. Set duration. Toggle sound.

Step 7. Hit generate.

2 prompts. 1 AI girl. 0 employees.

Meta spent $14B on Reality Labs last year to build digital humans. This creator built one in 7 minutes for less than a dinner.

The prompts are free in the reply.

items.title: TikTok's algorithm doesn't know she's not real.
items.summary:
TikTok's algorithm doesn't know she's not real.

It just sees a video about jelly bras getting clicks, watch time, and purchases.

$75,000 in revenue. From one AI-generated video copying the most profitable TikTok Shop post ever made.

Watch until 0:26. That's where he shows the number.

The original made $1.4 million. He took the blueprint and rebuilt it with Higgsfield, ElevenLabs, and Claude.

$50-$150/month in tools. $75,000 in commission.

TikTok Shop GMV hit $66 billion in 2026. The winning products pay up to 20% commission.

Find the top-performing video in a niche. Recreate it with an AI model. Test 10 hook variants. Post.

The algorithm rewards what works.

It doesn't ask who made it.

items.title: Top 10 robotics stories of May 2026 - The Robot Report
items.summary: # Top 10 robotics stories of May 2026. May 2026 was a busy month full of robotics news, topped off by last week’s Robotics Summit & Expo in Boston. The event brought together thousands of robotics experts to discuss the latest technology and industry advances. Here are the 10 most popular articles on *The Robot Report* in the past month. Subscribe to *The Robot Report*Newsletter and listen to *The Robot Report* Podcast to stay up to date on the latest robotics developments. Humanoid partners with Bosch, Schaeffler to scale robot production in May 2026. London-based Humanoid said it is working with Bosch to scale production after the successful completion of a joint proof of concept in March. It serves as the primary hub for NEO, the company’s humanoid robot designed to operate quietly—at a decibel level lower than a modern refrigerator—while navigating domestic spaces. If you’ve built a robotics company in the past 10 years, chances are you’ve at least flirted with the idea of robotics as a service, or RaaS.

items.title: ANSCER Robotics closes Series A round for industrial material handling - The Robot Report
items.summary: # ANSCER Robotics closes Series A round for industrial material handling. ANSCER Robotics provides autonomous mobile robots and software for industrial material handling. Aiming to capture a larger share of the American logistics market, deep-tech startup ANSCER Robotics has raised $5.4 million (₹45 crore) in Series A funding to scale its fleet of hybrid warehouse robots globally. The company said it is designing and building automation for the realities of modern manufacturing and logistics by helping enterprises improve productivity, safety, and operational efficiency at scale. According to ANSCER Robotics, this latest investment will accelerate its mission to build India’s AI-native industrial automation platform for factories and warehouses worldwide. “Naturally, we are expanding our markets and our product line, and the company will have a booth at Automate 2026,” Mark Messina, CEO of ANSCER Robotics Americas, told *The Robot Report*. “Our robots are designed and built for deployment in complex, rough environments — manufacturing floors and dock areas.”. Mike Oitzman is Senior Editor of WTWH's Robotics Group and founder of the Mobile Robot Guide.

items.title: Why warehouse robotics succeeds or fails at scale: A Q&A with Christina Gomez-Terry of Plus One Robotics - Robotics & Automation News
items.summary: # Why warehouse robotics succeeds or fails at scale: A Q&A with Christina Gomez-Terry of Plus One Robotics. **Warehouse automation has entered a new phase. As logistics operators expand robotics systems across multiple facilities, they often discover that success depends less on peak performance and more on consistency, reliability, and operational resilience. In this Q&A, Christina Gomez-Terry, vice president of operations at Plus One Robotics, discusses the practical realities of scaling warehouse automation. She also discusses the long-term role of human-in-the-loop operations, the importance of customer support infrastructure, the influence of ROS and open-source robotics on commercial systems, and why integration may become the next major bottleneck facing warehouse automation. **Robotics & Automation News: You’ve said that a robotics company is only as strong as its “worst day.” What are the most common operational problems that emerge when warehouse robotics systems move from pilot projects to large-scale deployments?**.

items.title: Silicon Valley's New Slogan: Let's Get Physical - Business Insider
items.summary: You're currently following this topic! The latest news and analysis on robotics, from humanoid AI to real-world automation. ### Figure AI had one of its robots race an intern to sort packages. This weekend offered a glimpse of how quickly the biggest AI players are moving in on humanoid robotics. Robotics has become tech's latest arms race, with Nvidia, OpenAI, Meta, Tesla, and a flock of startups racing to give AI a body. "Physical AI" is the buzzword of the moment in the Valley, a term popularized by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to describe AI systems that can act in the physical world. One of the buzziest Silicon Valley robotics upstarts, Figure AI, has been trying to prove its technology can move from demos to real work. The team is teaching a robotic arm how to perform household tasks as part of an effort to build a humanoid robot, Business Insider reported earlier this year.

items.title: Reservoir’s Danny Bernstein says we’re ‘entering the golden age of robotics’ in agriculture - AgFunderNews
items.summary: # Reservoir’s Danny Bernstein says we’re ‘entering the golden age of robotics’ in agriculture. #### **Reservoir's Danny Bernstein with Western Growers CEO and president Dave Puglia. With the cracks in chemical-based crop protection widening, many are now contemplating the role of farm robotics in helping growers reduce reliance on these inputs. Danny Bernstein, CEO and managing partner of farm robotics incubator Reservoir, says this thinking is top of mind for many of the startups now operating out of the company’s campuses, which are employing everything from zapping weeds via electricity to controlling pests with UV light. California-based Reservoir itself has now expanded to two campuses—one in the Salinas Valley and one in Sonoma County—and plans to break ground on a third, in Central Valley, later this summer. (The deal with Western Growers is for the incubator side of the business.) We also have a management company that runs our fund, and that’s Reservoir VC.

items.title: Personal update: I've joined Anthropic. I think the next few years at the frontier of LLMs will be especially formative.
items.summary: Personal update: I've joined Anthropic. I think the next few years at the frontier of LLMs will be especially formative. I am very excited to join the team here and get back to R&D. I remain deeply passionate about education and plan to resume my work on it in time.

items.title: This works really well btw, at the end of your query ask your LLM to "structure your response as HTML", then view the ge
items.summary:
This works really well btw, at the end of your query ask your LLM to "structure your response as HTML", then view the generated file in your browser. I've also had some success asking the LLM to present its output as slideshows, etc.

More generally, imo audio is the human-preferred input to AIs but vision (images/animations/video) is the preferred output from them. Around a ~third of our brains are a massively parallel processor dedicated to vision, it is the 10-lane superhighway of information into brain. As AI improves, I think we'll see a progression that takes advantage:

1) raw text (hard/effortful to read)
2) markdown (bold, italic, headings, tables, a bit easier on the eyes) <-- current default
3) HTML (still procedural with underlying code, but a lot more flexibility on the graphics, layout, even interactivity) <-- early but forming new good default
...4,5,6,...
n) interactive neural videos/simulations

Imo the extrapolation (though the technology doesn't exist just yet) ends in some kind of interactive videos generated directly by a diffusion neural net. Many open questions as to how exact/procedural "Software 1.0" artifacts (e.g. interactive simulations) may be woven together with neural artifacts (diffusion grids), but generally something in the direction of the recently viral https://t.co/z21CP5iQfu

There are also improvements necessary and pending at the input. Audio nor text nor video alone are not enough, e.g. I feel a need to point/gesture to things on the screen, similar to all the things you would do with a person physically next to you and your computer screen.

TLDR The input/output mind meld between humans and AIs is ongoing and there is a lot of work to do and significant progress to be made, way before jumping all the way into neuralink-esque BCIs and all that. For what's worth exploring at the current stage, hot tip try ask for HTML.
